items.question: Who uses derivatives in ML engineering?
items.answer: Anyone training models with gradient-based optimizers—frameworks compute derivatives of the loss with respect to billions of parameters automatically.

items.question: What is the formal definition of the derivative?
items.answer: f′(x) = lim_{h→0} (f(x+h)-f(x))/h when the limit exists.

items.question: When is the derivative zero at an interior point?
items.answer: At critical points—candidates for local maxima, minima or horizontal inflection—where the tangent is flat.

items.question: Where do derivatives appear in a training step?
items.answer: In backpropagation through each layer and in the optimizer when updating weights: θ ← θ - η∇L.

items.question: Why not use finite parameter search instead of derivatives?
items.answer: Derivatives give local direction and scale in O(1) extra work per parameter via autograd, whereas coordinate search is far slower in high dimensions.

items.question: How do you differentiate f(x) = x² sin x?
items.answer: Product rule: f′(x) = 2x sin x + x² cos x.
